LAW AND MOTION, 301, DEFENDANT ADAM CHREKI'S MOTION TO DEEM REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION ADMITTED AND FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS IS DENIED. CHREKI FILED HIS MOTION BEFORE THE EXTENSION OF TIME HE GAVE TO PLAINTIFF AMERICAN EXPRESS NATIONAL BANK TO RESPOND TO THE RFAS LAPSED. (CONLEY DECL., PARA. 8 & EX. 5.) COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: CHRISTINE VAN AKEN; CLERK: W. TRUPEK; NOT REPORTED (301/CVA)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Deem Requests For Admission Admitted And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Oct-24-2025 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT ADAM CHREKI'S Motion To Deem Requests For Admission Admitted And For Monetary Sanctions.

Defendant Adam Chreki's motion for a deemed-admitted order as to his first set of requests for admission is denied. Chreki filed his motion before the extension of time he gave to plaintiff American Express National Bank to respond to the RFAs lapsed. (Conley Decl., para. 8 & Ex. 5.)

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)
